Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.value: ADT with orteronel (300 mg oral twice daily)
Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: On page 1, in the 'METHODS' section, it states: 'In this open-label randomized phase III study, patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned 1:1 to ADT with orteronel (300 mg oral twice daily; experimental arm) or ADT with bicalutamide (50 mg oral once daily; control arm).' The experimental arm is Treatment Arm 1.

Number of Arms Included.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: 'In this open-label randomized phase III study, patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned 1:1 to ADT with orteronel (300 mg oral twice daily; experimental arm) or ADT with bicalutamide (50 mg oral once daily; control arm).' This indicates two arms: one experimental and one control.

Control Arm.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: 'In this open-label randomized phase III study, patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned 1:1 to ADT with orteronel (300 mg oral twice daily; experimental arm) or ADT with bicalutamide (50 mg oral once daily; control arm).' This clearly identifies 'ADT with bicalutamide' as the control arm.

Treatment Arm(s).evidence: On page 1, under the 'METHODS' section, it states: "patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned 1:1 to ADT with orteronel (300 mg oral twice daily; experimental arm) or ADT with bicalutamide (50 mg oral once daily; control arm)." The experimental arm is explicitly named here.

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: On page 1, in the 'PURPOSE' section, it states: 'Orteronel (TAK-700) is a nonsteroidal 17,20-lyase inhibitor suppressing androgen synthesis.' The 'METHODS' section on the same page identifies the experimental arm as 'ADT with orteronel'.
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

Treatment Arm - N.evidence: On page 1, in the 'RESULTS' section, it states: 'Among 1,279 patients included in the analysis, 638 were randomly assigned to the ADT plus orteronel arm and 641 to the control arm.' The 'ADT plus orteronel arm' is identified as the experimental arm in the 'METHODS' section. This is confirmed on page 4, in the 'Patients' section: 'Six hundred thirty-eight patients were randomly assigned to the orteronel arm'. Table 1 on page 5 also lists 'Orteronel (n = 638)'.

Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).evidence: On page 1, in the 'RESULTS' section, it states: 'After a median follow-up of 4.9 years, there was a significant improvement in PFS...'. This is confirmed on page 4, in the 'RESULTS' under 'Patients' section: 'At the cutoff date (February 5, 2021) for the final analysis after 525 deaths (and 787 PFS events), the median follow-up time was 4.9 years.' Figure 1 on page 3 also states 'Median follow-up: 4.9 years'. To convert 4.9 years to months, multiply by 12: 4.9 * 12 = 58.8 months.

Control Arm - N.evidence: In the 'RESULTS' section on page 1, it states: 'Among 1,279 patients included in the analysis, 638 were randomly assigned to the ADT plus orteronel arm and 641 to the control arm.' This value is also confirmed in Table 1 on page 5 under 'Bicalutamide (n = 641)' and in Figure 1 and Figure 2 as 'LHRHa plus bicalutamide' with N=641.

Total Participants - N.evidence: On page 1, in the 'RESULTS' section, it states: 'Among 1,279 patients included in the analysis, 638 were randomly assigned to the ADT plus orteronel arm and 641 to the control arm.' This is further confirmed on page 4, in the 'RESULTS' -> 'Patients' section, which states: '1,279 were included in the intention-to-treat analysis'. Figure A1 on page 13 also shows the ITT analysis population as 641 + 638 = 1,279.

Quality of Life reported.evidence: The document discusses primary and secondary endpoints such as overall survival (OS), progression-free survival (PFS), prostate-specific antigen (PSA) response rates, and adverse events (AEs). There is no mention of 'Quality of Life' or related terms being reported as an outcome or endpoint throughout the text, tables, or figures.

Trial.evidence: The name of the clinical study is explicitly stated in the title of the document: 'Orteronel for Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer: A Multicenter, Randomized, Open-Label Phase III Trial (SWOG-1216)'. It is also referred to as 'S1216 trial' in the 'Trial Design and Conduct' section on page 2 and 'SWOG S1216' in Table A2 on page 12.

Quality of Life Scale.evidence: The document describes primary and secondary endpoints such as overall survival (OS), progression-free survival (PFS), prostate-specific antigen (PSA) response rates, and adverse events (AEs). There is no mention of a 'Quality of Life Scale' or any measure of participants' overall well-being or satisfaction with their health status assessed through a standardized questionnaire or survey.

NCT.evidence: The NCT identifier 'NCT01809691' is found on page 8 under the 'CLINICAL TRIAL INFORMATION' section.

PubMed ID.evidence: A PubMed ID is not explicitly reported in the document. A DOI is present, but not a PubMed ID.

Trial Name.value: Orteronel for Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer: A Multicenter, Randomized, Open-Label Phase III Trial (SWOG-1216)
Trial Name.evidence: The trial name is the main title of the article, found on page 1.

Author.evidence: The first author, Neeraj Agarwal, and a list of co-authors are provided on page 1 under the title of the article. Using 'et al.' for brevity as allowed.

Year.evidence: The publication year '2022' is stated on page 1 under 'Accepted on March 18, 2022 and published at ascopubs.org/journal/jco on April 21, 2022'.

Full Pub or Abstract.evidence: The document is extensive, includes detailed methods, results, and discussion sections, and is labeled 'original reports' on page 1, indicating a full publication rather than just an abstract.

Phase.evidence: The title of the article on page 1 explicitly states 'Phase III Trial'. This is also confirmed in the 'METHODS' section on page 1 and 'Key Objective' on page 2.

Original/Follow Up.evidence: The document presents the primary findings of the SWOG-1216 trial, as indicated by the title and the comprehensive reporting of methods, results, and conclusions for this specific trial. It is not described as a follow-up analysis of a previously reported study.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Trea.evidence: The 'RESULTS' section on page 1 states: 'After a median follow-up of 4.9 years, there was a significant improvement in PFS (median 47.6 v 23.0 months)'. Figure 2 on page 4, titled 'Intention-to-treat comparison of progression-free survival by arm', explicitly shows 'LHRH agonist plus orteronel: median 47.6 months'. Progression-Free Survival (PFS) is a measure of the duration patients remain on treatment without disease progression or death, thus serving as the median on-treatment duration.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Cont.evidence: The 'RESULTS' section on page 1 states: 'After a median follow-up of 4.9 years, there was a significant improvement in PFS (median 47.6 v 23.0 months)'. Figure 2 on page 4, titled 'Intention-to-treat comparison of progression-free survival by arm', explicitly shows 'LHRH agonist plus bicalutamide: median 23.0 months'. Progression-Free Survival (PFS) is a measure of the duration patients remain on treatment without disease progression or death, thus serving as the median on-treatment duration.

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Treat.evidence: On page 5, in TABLE 1 'Baseline Characteristics of Patients Included in the Primary Intention-To-Treat Analysis', under the 'Orteronel (n = 638)' column and 'Disease severity, No. (%)' row, the 'Extensive' category shows '310 (49)'.

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Contr.evidence: On page 5, in TABLE 1 'Baseline Characteristics of Patients Included in the Primary Intention-To-Treat Analysis', under the 'Bicalutamide (n = 641)' column and 'Disease severity, No. (%)' row, the 'Extensive' category shows '313 (49)'.

Volume of disease - N (%) | Low | Contro.evidence: On page 5, in TABLE 1 'Baseline Characteristics of Patients Included in the Primary Intention-To-Treat Analysis', under the 'Bicalutamide (n = 641)' column and 'Disease severity, No. (%)' row, the 'Minimal' category shows '328 (51)'.

Median Age (years) | Treatment.evidence: The median age for the orteronel (treatment) arm is reported as 67.6 years in the 'RESULTS' -> 'Patients' section on page 4 and in Table 1, 'Baseline Characteristics of Patients Included in the Primary Intention-To-Treat Analysis', on page 5 under the 'Orteronel (n = 638)' column.

Median Age (years) | Control.evidence: The median age for the control (bicalutamide) arm is reported as 68.1 years in the 'RESULTS' -> 'Patients' section on page 4 and in Table 1, 'Baseline Characteristics of Patients Included in the Primary Intention-To-Treat Analysis', on page 5 under the 'Bicalutamide (n = 641)' column.

Primary Endpoint(s).evidence: On page 1, in the 'METHODS' section of the abstract, it states: 'The primary objective was the comparison of overall survival (OS)'. On page 3, under 'End Points', it further clarifies: 'The primary end point was OS, which was defined as the time from random assignment to the date of death from any cause.'

PS - N (%) | 0 | Treatment.evidence: Found in Table 1, page 5, under the 'Zubrod PS,ª No. (%)' section, row '0' for the 'Orteronel (n = 638)' (Treatment) column.

PS - N (%) | 0 | Control.evidence: Found in Table 1, page 5, under the 'Zubrod PS,ª No. (%)' section, row '0' for the 'Bicalutamide (n = 641)' (Control) column.

PS - N (%) | 1-2 | Treatment.evidence: Derived from Table 1, page 5, by summing the counts and percentages for 'Zubrod PS,ª No. (%)' rows '1' (197 (31%)) and '2' (21 (3%)) for the 'Orteronel (n = 638)' (Treatment) column. (197+21 = 218; 31%+3% = 34%).

PS - N (%) | 1-2 | Control.evidence: Derived from Table 1, page 5, by summing the counts and percentages for 'Zubrod PS,ª No. (%)' rows '1' (175 (27%)) and '2' (21 (3%)) for the 'Bicalutamide (n = 641)' (Control) column. (175+21 = 196; 27%+3% = 30%).

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.evidence: The study population consists of patients with "newly diagnosed metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer" (Page 1, Abstract), which implies synchronous metastases. However, the results are not explicitly reported by prognostic groups distinguishing between synchronous and metachronous metastases.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.evidence: The document does not mention metachronous disease or report results by prognostic groups based on metachronous metastases. The study focuses on "newly diagnosed metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer" (Page 1, Abstract).

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: The study used "severity of disease (minimal v extensive)" as a stratification factor (Page 2, "Patients and Interventions"). Table 1 ("Baseline Characteristics") reports patient numbers for "Extensive" disease. Figure 3 ("Estimated treatment HRs for overall survival by subgroup") includes "Extensive Disease" as a subgroup. Table A3 ("Treatment Outcomes by Disease Severity") explicitly reports PFS and OS outcomes for the "Extensive" group. Footnote b of Table A2 defines "extensive (high) risk" for S1216.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: The study used "severity of disease (minimal v extensive)" as a stratification factor (Page 2, "Patients and Interventions"). Table 1 ("Baseline Characteristics") reports patient numbers for "Minimal" disease. Figure 3 ("Estimated treatment HRs for overall survival by subgroup") includes "Minimal Disease" as a subgroup. Table A3 ("Treatment Outcomes by Disease Severity") explicitly reports PFS and OS outcomes for the "Minimal" group.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: The document describes the treatment arm as 'ADT with orteronel' and the control arm as 'ADT with bicalutamide' (Page 2, 'METHODS'). Docetaxel is not mentioned as part of the protocol treatment for either arm. The 'Patients and Interventions' section (Page 2) states that 'No other prior systemic therapy for metastatic prostate cancer was allowed (with the exception of up to 30 days of ADT for metastatic disease before registration)', indicating prior docetaxel was not permitted. It also mentions that patients 'unsuitable candidates for docetaxel or if they declined docetaxel therapy' were eligible, further confirming docetaxel was not part of the study's intervention. Docetaxel is mentioned as a 'subsequent life-prolonging therapy' received *after* discontinuation of protocol therapy (Page 6, 'DISCUSSION'), but this is outside the scope of 'before or during the trial'.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.evidence: The document describes the treatment arm as 'ADT with orteronel' and the control arm as 'ADT with bicalutamide' (Page 2, 'METHODS'). Docetaxel is not mentioned as part of the protocol treatment for either arm. The 'Patients and Interventions' section (Page 2) states that 'No other prior systemic therapy for metastatic prostate cancer was allowed (with the exception of up to 30 days of ADT for metastatic disease before registration)', indicating prior docetaxel was not permitted. It also mentions that patients 'unsuitable candidates for docetaxel or if they declined docetaxel therapy' were eligible, further confirming docetaxel was not part of the study's intervention. Docetaxel is mentioned as a 'subsequent life-prolonging therapy' received *after* discontinuation of protocol therapy (Page 6, 'DISCUSSION'), but this is outside the scope of 'before or during the trial'.

TTPSA (mo) | Control.evidence: On page 4, under 'Secondary End Points', it states: 'compared with 23.0 months in the control group'. PFS is defined on page 3 as 'time from random assignment to first documentation of PSA progression', which aligns with TTPSA.

Treatment Class.value: Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) and Androgen Axis Inhibition
Treatment Class.evidence: The 'PURPOSE' section on page 1 states the study evaluated orteronel when added to 'androgen deprivation therapy (ADT)'. The 'METHODS' section on page 1 describes participants being assigned to 'ADT with orteronel' or 'ADT with bicalutamide'. The 'Key Objective' on page 2 further clarifies that orteronel is a 'novel androgen axis inhibitor' and the study compares it to ADT plus bicalutamide.

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.evidence: The document is a report of a single randomized controlled trial (RCT) and does not contain an explicit 'overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence (COE)' in the sense of a systematic assessment of evidence quality or risk of bias for various criteria within this RCT or across multiple RCTs. While it discusses the study's findings and implications, it does not provide this specific type of judgment.

Add-on Treatment.evidence: The "PURPOSE" section on page 1 states, "We evaluated the clinical benefit of orteronel when added to androgen deprivation therapy (ADT)...". The "METHODS" section on page 1 further clarifies that patients were assigned to "ADT with orteronel" (experimental arm) or "ADT with bicalutamide" (control arm). Orteronel is the add-on treatment being investigated in the experimental arm.

Median OS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: Found in the 'RESULTS' section on page 1: 'median 81.1 v 70.2 months'. The 81.1 months corresponds to the orteronel arm (treatment). This is confirmed in FIG 1 on page 3, which states 'LHRH agonist plus orteronel: median 81.1 months', and in the 'Primary End Point' section on page 4: 'The median OS in the orteronel group was 81.1 months'.

Median OS (mo) | Overall | Control.evidence: Found in the 'RESULTS' section on page 1: 'median 81.1 v 70.2 months'. The 70.2 months corresponds to the control arm. This is confirmed in FIG 1 on page 3, which states 'LHRH agonist plus bicalutamide: median 70.2 months', and in the 'Primary End Point' section on page 4: 'as compared with 70.2 months in the control group'.

ORR - N (%) | Treatment | Overall.evidence: The document reports 'PSA response rates' for the treatment arm as CR 58%, PR: 22%, NR: 19% on page 4 under 'Secondary End Points'. However, it does not report the Overall Response Rate (CR+PR) based on metastatic disease (tumor) response, which is distinct from PSA response.

ORR - N (%) | Treatment | CR.evidence: The document reports 'PSA response rates' for the treatment arm as CR 58% on page 4 under 'Secondary End Points'. However, it does not report the Complete Response (CR) based on metastatic disease (tumor) response, which is distinct from PSA response.

ORR - N (%) | Treatment | SD.evidence: The document reports 'PSA response rates' for the treatment arm, including NR (No Response) 19%, on page 4 under 'Secondary End Points'. However, it does not report the Stable Disease (SD) based on metastatic disease (tumor) response, which is distinct from PSA response. PD is mentioned in the context of PFS, but not as a percentage of patients with stable disease.

Type of Therapy.value: ADT with orteronel (300 mg oral twice daily; experimental arm) or ADT with bicalutamide (50 mg oral once daily; control arm).
Type of Therapy.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: 'In this open-label randomized phase III study, patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned 1:1 to ADT with orteronel (300 mg oral twice daily; experimental arm) or ADT with bicalutamide (50 mg oral once daily; control arm).'

OS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: Found in the 'Primary End Point' section on page 4: 'The OS rate at 5 years was 59.7% in the orteronel group'.

OS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.evidence: Found in the 'Primary End Point' section on page 4: 'The OS rate at 5 years was ... 57.9% in the control group'.

OS Rate (%) | High volume | Treatment.evidence: Found in TABLE A3 'Treatment Outcomes by Disease Severity' on page 13, under 'Extensive' disease group, 'OS Orteronel Median/5 Year' column, reported as '4.8 years/47%'. 'Extensive' disease is considered high volume.

OS Rate (%) | High volume | Control.evidence: Found in TABLE A3 'Treatment Outcomes by Disease Severity' on page 13, under 'Extensive' disease group, 'OS Control Median/5 Year' column, reported as '4.1 years/44%'. 'Extensive' disease is considered high volume.

OS Rate (%) | Low volume | Treatment.evidence: Found in TABLE A3 'Treatment Outcomes by Disease Severity' on page 13, under 'Minimal' disease group, 'OS Orteronel Median/5 Year' column, reported as 'NR/71%'. 'Minimal' disease is considered low volume.

OS Rate (%) | Low volume | Control.evidence: Found in TABLE A3 'Treatment Outcomes by Disease Severity' on page 13, under 'Minimal' disease group, 'OS Control Median/5 Year' column, reported as 'NR/71%'. 'Minimal' disease is considered low volume.

Median PFS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: The value '47.6 months' for median PFS in the orteronel group (experimental arm) is found in the 'RESULTS' section on page 1 and the 'Secondary End Points' section on page 4, and also visually represented in Figure 2 on page 4 as 'LHRH agonist plus orteronel: median 47.6 months'.

Median PFS (mo) | Overall | Control.evidence: The value '23.0 months' for median PFS in the control group is found in the 'RESULTS' section on page 1 and the 'Secondary End Points' section on page 4, and also visually represented in Figure 2 on page 4 as 'LHRH agonist plus bicalutamide: median 23.0 months'.

Median PFS (mo) | High volume | Treatmen.evidence: The document defines 'extensive disease' as high volume on page 2. In Table A3 on page 13, under 'Extensive' disease group, the 'PFS Orteronel Median/5 Year' is '2.2 years'. Converting 2.2 years to months: 2.2 * 12 = 26.4 months.

Median PFS (mo) | High volume | Control.evidence: The document defines 'extensive disease' as high volume on page 2. In Table A3 on page 13, under 'Extensive' disease group, the 'PFS Control Median/5 Year' is '1.1 years'. Converting 1.1 years to months: 1.1 * 12 = 13.2 months.

Median PFS (mo) | Low volume | Treatment.evidence: The document defines 'minimal disease' as low volume on page 2. In Table A3 on page 13, under 'Minimal' disease group, the 'PFS Orteronel Median/5 Year' is 'NR' (Not Reached).

Median PFS (mo) | Low volume | Control.evidence: The document defines 'minimal disease' as low volume on page 2. In Table A3 on page 13, under 'Minimal' disease group, the 'PFS Control Median/5 Year' is '3.6 years'. Converting 3.6 years to months: 3.6 * 12 = 43.2 months.

Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.evidence: Found in the 'RESULTS' section on page 1: 'More grade 3/4 adverse events occurred in the experimental versus the control arms (43% v 14%)' and confirmed in the 'Safety' section on page 4: 'More grade 3 and 4 AEs occurred in the orteronel group versus the control group (43% v13%)'. The 43% refers to the orteronel (treatment) arm. The exact count (N) for this summary percentage is not explicitly stated.

Found in the 'RESULTS' section on page 1: 'More grade 3/4 adverse events occurred in the experimental versus the control arms (43% v 14%)' and confirmed in the 'Safety' section on page 4: 'More grade 3 and 4 AEs occurred in the orteronel group versus the control group (43% v13%)'. The 13% refers to the control arm. The exact count (N) for this summary percentage is not explicitly stated.

Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.evidence: Derived from TABLE 2 on page 7, 'No. and Percent of Patients With a Given Type and Grade of AE'. The footnote states 'AEs unlikely or not related to treatment were excluded', implying the listed AEs are treatment-related. Summing the 'Grade 3-5' counts for each AE in the 'LHRHa Plus Orteronel (n = 627)' column gives 235. The percentage is (235 / 627) * 100 = 37.48%, rounded to 37.5%.

Derived from TABLE 2 on page 7, 'No. and Percent of Patients With a Given Type and Grade of AE'. The footnote states 'AEs unlikely or not related to treatment were excluded', implying the listed AEs are treatment-related. Summing the 'Grade 3-5' counts for each AE in the 'LHRHa Plus Bicalutamide (n = 629)' column gives 36. The percentage is (36 / 629) * 100 = 5.72%, rounded to 5.7%.

Found in the 'Safety' section on page 4: 'Five patients in the experimental arm... had grade 5 AEs'. The footnote of TABLE 2 on page 7 further details these Grade 5 AEs and states that 'AEs unlikely or not related to treatment were excluded', implying these are treatment-related. The total N for the Orteronel arm is 627 (from Table 2). The percentage is (5 / 627) * 100 = 0.797%, rounded to 0.8%.

Found in the 'Safety' section on page 4: '...one patient in the control arm had grade 5 AEs'. The footnote of TABLE 2 on page 7 further details this Grade 5 AE and states that 'AEs unlikely or not related to treatment were excluded', implying this is treatment-related. The total N for the Bicalutamide arm is 629 (from Table 2). The percentage is (1 / 629) * 100 = 0.159%, rounded to 0.2%.
